jokes.text: Only on your brain.
jokes.setup: Jim asks 'Is the fuzz still here?' suggesting paranoia about police
jokes.punchline: Response that the only 'fuzz' is on Jim's brain, implying he's confused/high
jokes.explanation: Quick, efficient wordplay on 'fuzz' that establishes Jim's character while deflating his paranoia

jokes.text: Well, I've been in the bathroom.
jokes.setup: Investigator says Jim has been 'a hard man to find'
jokes.punchline: Jim's matter-of-fact response that he's just been in the bathroom
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jim's oblivious nature and literal thinking. Great economy of words.

jokes.text: Yeah. You know... it was the '60s
jokes.setup: Someone asks why Jim changed his name to Ignatowski
jokes.punchline: Jim's casual dismissal as if the decade explains everything
jokes.explanation: Captures the absurd cultural logic of the hippie era perfectly. Jim's delivery would be key here.

jokes.text: Ikswotangi. Uh-oh. That's not even close to 'Star Child,' is it?
jokes.setup: Jim tells someone to say 'Ignatowski' backwards, expecting it to spell something meaningful
jokes.punchline: The backwards version makes no sense and Jim realizes it doesn't spell what he intended
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Jim's drug-addled logic failing in real time. The self-awareness makes it funnier.

jokes.text: It's written in some strange language unknown on this planet.
jokes.setup: Jim receives a letter and examines it
jokes.punchline: Jim describes normal handwriting as an alien language
jokes.explanation: Good character consistency showing Jim's disconnect from reality, though the concept is fairly predictable

jokes.text: That's okay. I didn't even hear it.
jokes.setup: Someone apologizes for intruding
jokes.punchline: Jim says he didn't hear the intrusion, missing the point entirely
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat but feels like a standard 'spaced out character' joke without much surprise

jokes.text: Jim, that's Bonanza. Oh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Jim describes his brothers 'Little Joe and Hoss' as if they're real family members
jokes.punchline: Someone points out these are TV characters, Jim casually acknowledges the mistake
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Jim's tendency to confuse his life with TV shows. His casual acceptance sells it.

jokes.text: You know, once a month like clockwork, I call him on the phone and hang up just so I can hear his voice say 'For the last time, who is this?'
jokes.setup: Jim mentions it's terrible to forget the kind of people you come from
jokes.punchline: Jim's bizarre method of staying connected to his father through prank calls
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that's both pathetic and endearing. Shows Jim's broken logic and emotional needs perfectly.

jokes.text: Okay. Now, now, now let's not fight over this.
jokes.setup: Multiple people decline Jim's offer to accompany him
jokes.punchline: Jim acts like people are fighting over the opportunity when nobody wants it
jokes.explanation: Good character obliviousness but the setup makes it somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Boy, what are the odds of that happening?
jokes.setup: Everyone picks the number ten when Jim is thinking of three
jokes.punchline: Jim marvels at the 'coincidence' as if it's shocking rather than obvious strategic thinking
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's mathematical obliviousness but the joke is fairly straightforward

jokes.text: Ignatowski's got a father? There goes my spore theory.
jokes.setup: Jim mentions he's going to see his father
jokes.punchline: Louie's shock that Jim has a father, joking he thought Jim reproduced asexually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie line - cruel, scientifically absurd, and perfectly delivered. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: I think he's a butler. I don't care what he does for a living. He's my father.
jokes.setup: Elaine identifies the man who answered the door as a butler
jokes.punchline: Jim assumes the butler is his father and doesn't care about the job
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's confusion about social class and his touching acceptance

jokes.text: Tom Caldwell, the son that lived.
jokes.setup: Jim introduces Tom as his brother
jokes.punchline: Tom's darkly matter-of-fact self-introduction implying Jim is considered dead to the family
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny establishment of family dynamics. Efficient and cuts deep without being over-the-top.

jokes.text: Lila, I'd like you to meet my brother, Tom-Jim.
jokes.setup: Jim introduces his sister Lila to Tom
jokes.punchline: Jim gets confused and calls Tom 'Tom-Jim' as if that's his actual name
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Jim's confusion but feels like a lesser version of his other mix-ups

jokes.text: Want a bite?
jokes.setup: Lila is introduced as Jim's sister
jokes.punchline: Her first words are offering food in an inappropriate social context
jokes.explanation: Establishes Lila's odd social behavior efficiently but the joke itself is mild

jokes.text: Promise me you won't leave me alone with them.
jokes.setup: Alex has been observing Jim's bizarre family
jokes.punchline: Alex's desperate plea showing how overwhelming the family is
jokes.explanation: Good audience surrogate moment but predictable response to the established weirdness

jokes.text: But she never took off those white gloves.
jokes.setup: Jim looks at a portrait and says he doesn't remember his mother much
jokes.punchline: Jim's oddly specific 'memory' about white gloves that probably weren't real
jokes.explanation: Nicely weird detail that could be real memory or TV/movie confusion, keeping us guessing

jokes.text: For some reason, she used to call me Miss Scarlett. Jim, that's Gone With the Wind. Yeah... no use looking back on it now.
jokes.setup: Jim describes his nanny calling him Miss Scarlett
jokes.punchline: Alex identifies this as another movie reference, Jim dismisses it casually
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the running gag. Jim's philosophical dismissal 'no use looking back' is perfect absurdist capping.

jokes.text: Jim, that's Gone With the Wind. Yeah... no use looking back on it now.
jokes.setup: Alex points out Jim is describing a movie
jokes.punchline: Jim's philosophical dismissal as if it doesn't matter
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Jim's consistent inability to distinguish fiction from memory

jokes.text: This could be my dad... or your dad.
jokes.setup: Someone approaches who might be Jim's father
jokes.punchline: Jim suggests the person could be anyone's father, showing complete disorientation
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's confusion but feels like a weaker version of his other disoriented moments

jokes.text: You're bald.
jokes.setup: Jim reunites with his father after many years
jokes.punchline: Jim's first observation is bluntly pointing out his father's baldness
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Jim's complete lack of social awareness at a poignant family moment

jokes.text: Thought about a toup?
jokes.setup: Father tries to have a meaningful conversation about the past
jokes.punchline: Jim continues fixating on his father's baldness, suggesting a toupee
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the baldness fixation, maintaining the cringe while his father tries to be sincere

jokes.text: Well, what about the fat part?
jokes.setup: Alex tries to redirect conversation from baldness to appearance being distinguished
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately pivots to commenting on his father's weight
jokes.explanation: Continues the cringe escalation effectively but feels somewhat predictable after the baldness comments

jokes.text: Oh, yes! Now I remember. Everybody here was fat.
jokes.setup: Father says he's the same size as when Jim left
jokes.punchline: Jim's 'fond' memory that everyone in the family was overweight
jokes.explanation: Consistent with Jim's oblivious tactlessness and adds to the family dynamics revelation

jokes.text: My God, I-I've been estranged from the wrong family.
jokes.setup: Jim reveals he's been calling but hanging up, explaining wrong numbers
jokes.punchline: Father realizes the cosmic joke of being estranged due to Jim calling wrong numbers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist payoff that recontextualizes both Jim's pathetic gesture and his father's confusion. Great irony.

jokes.text: You finished all of that.
jokes.setup: Father mentions Jim's toys are still in his room except the airplane glue
jokes.punchline: Casual acknowledgment that Jim used all the glue for drug purposes
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly understated reference to Jim's drug history. The father's matter-of-fact delivery sells it.

jokes.text: I don't love my brother, Alex. That's all right, Jim. I don't know why. He's almost as fat as my dad.
jokes.setup: Jim confesses he doesn't love his brother
jokes.punchline: His explanation is that his brother is almost as fat as his dad, showing twisted priorities
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's completely warped emotional priorities where physical appearance matters more than family bonds

jokes.text: Everything but string beans, sir.
jokes.setup: Asked what else they're having for dinner
jokes.punchline: Butler's oddly specific exclusion of one random vegetable
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing randomness but doesn't feel particularly earned or character-specific

jokes.text: Lila, don't be cheap in front of the company.
jokes.setup: Lila offers food to Alex
jokes.punchline: Father scolds her for being 'cheap' in an elaborate dining setting
jokes.explanation: Shows family dynamics and priorities but the joke lacks punch

jokes.text: A typewriter seemed so impersonal.
jokes.setup: Father explains Jim wrote term papers in finger paint at Harvard
jokes.punchline: Jim's artistic justification for this clearly inappropriate academic choice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim logic - taking hippie philosophy to absurd academic extremes. Shows his character's consistency across time.

jokes.text: Well, don't make it sound so terrible. How do you make that sound good? Well, drifting... You know, living on handouts. And getting stoned.
jokes.setup: Father lists Jim's failures, Jim says don't make it sound terrible
jokes.punchline: Jim repeats the exact same list as if it's positive, with no changes
jokes.explanation: Masterful absurdist moment showing Jim's complete inability to see problems as problems. Perfect structure and timing.

jokes.text: What do you feel? Full.
jokes.setup: Father asks what Jim feels after listing shame, remorse
jokes.punchline: Jim's completely literal, physical response to an emotional question
jokes.explanation: Perfect character beat showing Jim's inability to engage emotionally. Great economy and perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Okeydoke.
jokes.setup: Father dramatically announces Jim is getting no inheritance
jokes.punchline: Jim's completely casual, unbothered response to being disinherited
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understated reaction that completely deflates the father's dramatic moment. Shows Jim's genuine indifference to money.

jokes.text: Like, I wonder about if you call an orange an orange why don't you call a banana a yellow? Or an apple a red? Well, you see, the point is, Mr. Caldwell... Now, blueberries make sense, but somebody explain gooseberries to me.
jokes.setup: Jim tries to explain his philosophical reasons for coming home
jokes.punchline: Jim gets sidetracked into fruit-naming philosophy, confusing everyone including Alex
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim digression showing his thought process derailing. The fruit logic is genuinely absurd and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Give it all away to what's his name... The big guy with the curly hair. Your brother. Or him.
jokes.setup: Father asks if Jim really doesn't care about the money
jokes.punchline: Jim casually suggests giving it to his brother, whom he can't even remember the name of
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's genuine disinterest in money and family, consistent with character but not especially surprising

jokes.text: Good-bye, Dad.
jokes.setup: Father offers Jim a chance to come home, get education, live normally
jokes.punchline: Jim immediately rejects this 'generous' offer and leaves
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jim values his freedom over conventional success. Economical and earned.

jokes.text: And get his right phone number!
jokes.setup: Jim is leaving and asks Alex to say goodbye to his father
jokes.punchline: Jim's reminder to get the correct number, referencing the earlier wrong number revelation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Jim learned something practical from the visit while maintaining his character priorities
